title: bud.bats: fix expected aarch
body:
buildah inspect prints out arm image architecture as `aarch64` while expected architecture was set to `arm64`. This was causing gating test failures on Fedora bodhi.

Ref: https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/6d9c4f78-7ec3-4911-bea0-77395b8b19f3/

Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>


@flouthoc @nalind @edsantiago @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL

Fixes a buildah inspect test for aarch64 images.

#### How to verify it

Oh but just wondering how does it passes in buildah CI then. Checking this.

I've built buildah from `main` and on my local the test is passing without any changes, Checking why this is showing unexpected output on gaiting tests. I think buildah CI will fail with this change if i am correct.

fwiw, this seems to have also passed on F36 https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/1e51e396-ff17-4263-91bb-a52d678a7903/ . Wonder if something changed on rawhide. Will take a while to get results on Fedora 37
You're fixing the fix: Everything broke, #4377 fixed it.

> You're fixing the fix: Everything broke, #4377 fixed it.

lol alright. I'll build buildah with that patch, thanks
